extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds. The only transition that can set sent:=true while opted_out is true is [t4] (send_attempt if opted_out and acc==10). All other relevant transitions set sent:=false when opted_out: t1 (optout) sets sent:=false, t3 (opted_out, acc!=10) sets sent:=false, and step_0/step_1/step_2 set sent:=false. t2 only fires when not opted_out. So a violation requires reaching opted_out=true with acc==10. acc starts at 0 and is only changed by step events, whose decoded update functions are step_0/step_1: acc:=(3*acc+13) mod 16, and step_2: acc:=(3*acc+9) mod 16 (the nested if-subtractions implement mod 16). From acc=0, the reachable acc set is exactly {0,1,4,5,8,9,12,13} (all values ≡0 or 1 mod 4); the residue-class structure (3*acc keeps mod-4 class, adding 13≡1 or 9≡1 mod 4 keeps it in {0,1} mod 4) makes acc==10 (≡2 mod 4) unreachable forever. Therefore t4 is never enabled, so sent is never set to true while opted_out is true. An exhaustive BFS over the full (opted_out, sent, acc) state space (24 reachable states, within the 18-event bound) found no state with opted_out and sent both true. Property holds; both witness arrays empty.
